title: 6. I Am Reality | Do We Get to Win This Time?
format: The Vietnam-on-film series reaches Platoon: how Oliver Stone survived Salvador, ran his cast through Dale Dye's jungle boot camp, and turned his own infantry tour into the 1986 Best Picture winner.

segments.heading: How Hollywood Made the Vietnam War
segments.note: Oliver Stone and the making of Platoon

films.title: Platoon
films.note: Stone's alter-ego Chris Taylor (Charlie Sheen, cast after brother Emilio Estevez aged out) between Barnes (Tom Berenger) and Elias (Willem Dafoe). Shot in 54 days in the Philippines for $6.5M; eight Oscar nominations, wins for Best Picture and Best Director. Keith David plays King.

films.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II
films.note: Took over the summer-1985 box office; its 'we get to win this time' jingoism was exactly what Stone set out to rebut.

films.title: Missing in Action
films.note: Grouped with Rambo as the POW-rescue films selling US troops re-fighting and winning Vietnam.

films.title: Midnight Express
films.note: The prison thriller that won Stone a screenwriting Oscar, though it never made him feel accepted in Hollywood.

films.title: Scarface
films.note: Stone's script for Al Pacino's remake, cited as proof of his A-list screenwriting resume.

films.title: Seizure
films.note: Stone's early horror film, with Herve Villechaize as a figure named Spider; part of the thin directing resume he had before Salvador.

films.title: The Hand
films.note: Stone's other early horror film, Michael Caine as an illustrator whose severed hand takes on a life of its own.

films.title: Salvador
films.note: The James Woods El Salvador thriller Stone directed first, financed by John Daly's Hemdale; pulling it off cheap is what got Platoon greenlit.

films.title: The Deer Hunter
films.note: Michael Cimino briefly wanted to produce Platoon; cited as the earlier Vietnam film that fixed war in a game of Russian roulette.

films.title: Apocalypse Now
films.note: Martin Sheen starred; Charlie Sheen visited the set as a kid. Coppola's rainy-season Philippines ordeal was the cautionary tale Stone raced to avoid.

films.title: The Big Chill
films.note: Tom Berenger came off it as a nice-guy TV star before Stone turned him into the fatalistic Sergeant Barnes.

films.title: To Live and Die in L.A.
films.note: Dafoe had just played its sinister counterfeiter, against type from the paternal Elias.

films.title: Sands of Iwo Jima
films.note: John Wayne WWII film, a favorite of technical advisor Dale Dye growing up in Missouri.

films.title: Saving Private Ryan
films.note: Later film Dye ran military prep on, after his Platoon boot camp made the practice standard.

films.title: Forrest Gump
films.note: Another Dye military-advising credit named alongside his later work.

films.title: The Thin Red Line
films.note: Named with Saving Private Ryan and Forrest Gump as films Dye prepared with military training.

films.title: Top Gun
films.note: The year's biggest hit, a flag-wrapped ode to US might; Platoon's muddy counterweight.
